title: Apple333 security phase
objective: Assess or harden one approved security control with a documented threat model, least-privilege scope, and no production access.

inScope: Review an approved authentication, authorization, validation, logging, secret-handling, or deployment control.
Add targeted local tests and security documentation.
outOfScope: Production penetration testing or credential access.

decisions.title: Fail closed and minimize disclosure
decisions.rationale: Security controls should reject unsafe inputs, avoid exposing implementation details, and preserve auditability.

dependencies: Security owner approval for policy changes

steps.description: Record assets, trust boundaries, abuse cases, and expected controls for the approved scope.
steps.expectedOutcome: A concise threat model with explicit assumptions.

steps.description: Implement only the approved local control and test its failure modes.
steps.expectedOutcome: A narrowly scoped security improvement with regression coverage.

steps.description: Verify errors, logs, and documentation do not disclose secrets or sensitive implementation detail.

nonGoals: Do not access live credentials or production user data.
Do not perform destructive or exploitative testing.

commands.purpose: Verify lint and security-oriented code conventions.

manualChecks: Review threat model assumptions and residual risk with the security owner.
Confirm all logs and documentation avoid secret values.

deliverables.description: Captures threats, controls, validation evidence, and residual risks.

risks.description: A security change can break authentication, authorization, or operational visibility if its failure modes are not tested.
risks.mitigation: Use fail-closed behavior, focused regression tests, and security-owner review before release.

acceptanceCriteria.statement: The approved security control is documented, tested, and fails safely.
No production access, credential handling, destructive action, or database change occurs.

stopConditions: Stop if validation requires a live credential, production target, or unapproved data access.
Stop if remediation exceeds the documented security scope.
